title: feat(processors): add onDetection callback to PromptInjectionDetector and PIIDetector
summary: Adds an optional `onDetection` callback to both `PromptInjectionDetector` and `PIIDetector` processors, allowing consumers to observe detection results (what was detected, which strategy was applied, original input) without subclassing. Includes comprehensive tests for callback invocation, async support, and multi-message handling. Also bundles an unrelated fix for `data-*` chunks from `writer.custom()` not passing through output processors (separate changeset, touches `tool-stream.test.ts` and `stream.ts`). Code was generated with GitHub Copilot agent. Fixes #13336.
maintainerNotes: Maintainer DanielSLew commented (Feb 24) requesting a design change: the `onDetection` callback should be generalized to work with ANY processor (receiving the processor id and appropriate metadata), not just hardcoded into PIIDetector and PromptInjectionDetector. The author has not responded to this feedback in 14 days. Additionally: (1) the PR bundles an unrelated fix for data-* chunks bypassing output processors, which should ideally be a separate PR; (2) CI checks are stuck in PENDING state (likely needs maintainer authorization to trigger workflows for external contributor); (3) CodeRabbit flagged that onDetection errors are misattributed as detection failures (should be isolated in try/catch), and that strategyApplied may be inaccurate when strategy falls back (e.g., rewrite->filter). These are valid concerns that need addressing.
